answers.answer: D) Pause the discussion and explicitly invite input: "I'd like to hear from everyone. [Name], you've been working closest to this module - what's your take?" Reason: Option A excludes quieter voices entirely. Option B puts burden on quiet members, not inclusive. Option C is too late - decision already made. Option D is best: proactively creates safe space, specifically invites based on relevant expertise, ensures everyone feels included.

Slack DM to Dana (Sunday evening):

"Hi Dana! Welcome back! Hope you had a restful few months. Just wanted to give you a quick heads-up before Monday: no rush to dive in headfirst. Let's plan a gentle ramp-up - maybe start with catching up on project docs and joining standups, and we can schedule a 1:1 Monday afternoon to go over anything you want to prioritize. The team's excited to have you back! If you need anything - flexible hours, lighter load, whatever - just let me know. We'll figure it out together. See you Monday!"

Team message in #engineering:

"@channel Hey team! Dana is back starting Monday - welcome back! Dana, we've got some updates to catch you up on, but no rush. Let's give you space to settle back in. Everyone, please say hi and feel free to share any relevant context Dana might need as they ramp up. Looking forward to having the full team back together!"
